item.title: Here’s What Real GFiber Customers Think About Their Internet
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: On the fence about Google Fiber? Learn what real customers think about Google Fiber and why it’s been a top ISP of CableTV.com readers for several years running.
item.content:encoded:
If you’ve recently been shopping for internet service, you could be forgiven if GFiber (formerly Google Fiber) sounded too good to be true. While the industry has been defined by data caps and hidden fees, GFiber was built to prove that incredibly fast, reliable home internet doesn’t have to come with a catch. Since 2012, GFiber has been building a high-speed fiber network to serve homes and businesses across the nation. Experts and customers agree that GFiber offers a stellar mix of fast fiber internet speeds, simple pricing, and stress-free installation. Need more examples of why real GFiber internet customers love their service? Keep on reading to learn more.

Why customers and experts love GFiber

GFiber is consistently ranked among the nation’s best ISPs by multiple sources.

  • Since becoming eligible for the JD Power Customer Satisfaction Award in 2023, GFiber has been ranked No. 1 for Residential Wired Internet Service in the South Region for three consecutive years (2023, 2024, and 2025). GFiber has swept every sub-category each year, including the highest in value for price paid.
  • GFiber was named the three-time winner of Best Overall Internet Provider in the U.S. by HighSpeedInternet.com in 2024, 2025, and 2026.
  • Additionally, we here at CableTV.com named GFiber as our top internet provider for 2025 and 2026.

On top of all that, CableTV.com’s readers are also huge GFiber fans. As part of our annual Internet Customer Satisfaction Survey series, we interviewed more than 8,000 home internet customers to see what they think about their ISPs. According to CableTV.com’s 2026 survey, GFiber ranked first overall in multiple categories among fiber and cable ISPs, including:

  • No. 1 in overall customer satisfaction
  • No. 1 in bang for your buck value
  • No. 1 in willingness to recommend GFiber to others

Check out these reviews from real GFiber customers we talked to.

What customers say about GFiber

Wonderful, fast, and reliable service. We game in our home, and we use about two gaming systems, the TV, home security, plus smart home things in addition to our cellphones all at the same time seamlessly. When there is a service disruption, they auto-credit our account so we don't have to call for a discount on the occasional, very rare outage. It's the best internet service we have ever had.

GFiber customer in Georgia

I have had almost no issues whatsoever with GFiber. There were two days where service went out, and it was frustrating, but timely updates on the progress was accessible. Their customer service was respectful and responsive. Overall, it has met my expectations, the cost is reasonable, and I never have surprise rate increases. Additionally, I am able to get a copy of our conversations, which a previous service refused to do. Therefore, my trust in the company is a 10/10.

GFiber customer in California

The rebates for down internet time is nice. I have not had any outages or other issues after having the service for almost a year. The people who came to route our wires was very friendly. The cost is comparable and mostly better than other companies.

GFiber customer in Kansas

Consistent service without any interruptions or issues so far. The bill is as advertised without additional hidden fees.

GFiber customer in California

How do you get GFiber?

Starting to consider GFiber? Their home internet extends across 19 states.

Alabama Arizona California Colorado Florida Georgia Idaho
Illinois Iowa Kansas Missouri Nebraska Nevada
North Carolina South Carolina Tennessee Texas Utah Washington
On top of that, GFiber is constantly adding new regions to its GFiber network. If they haven’t reached your neighborhood just yet, you can sign up for email notifications to see when they’re breaking ground on new fiber connections.
Learn More About GFiber

item.title: 2025’s Best Musical Revival Wasn’t Nominated for a Tony—But It Is Getting a Proshot
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: The apotheosis is upon us. Find out when ‘TGWDLM: Reprised!’ is available to buy and when it hits YouTube for free.
item.content:encoded:
Goin’ back to Hatchetfield

Team StarKid, the comedy musical troupe behind viral hits like “A Very Potter Musical,” “Holy Musical B@man,” and that one song about not doing work that was on your TikTok feed for like a month, is back at it. And I mean really back at it—they’re reproducing one of their best musicals. StarKid first released “The Guy Who Didn’t Like Musicals” on YouTube in late 2018. The show, a horror-comedy about a body-snatching alien invasion, was an instant hit with the group’s fans and spawned a whole universe of horror comedies taking place in the same cursed town of Hatchetfield. But StarKid’s production value has increased a lot since that original production, and now they’re revisiting “TGWDLM” with a way bigger budget. That original production fundraised a budget of $127,792 on Kickstarter back in 2018. This new project, which ran a Kickstarter campaign in 2025, raised $642,019. That’s more than any StarKid show has ever raised on the platform. So you can expect this revival, “The Guy Who Didn’t Like Musicals: Reprised!,” to be even bigger and better. Most of the original cast is returning, with Will Branner from “Nerdy Prudes Must Die” subbing in as Professor Hidgens. So how can you watch “TGWDLM: Reprised!” if you didn’t see it live? Don’t worry; like all StarKid shows, it’s available as an official proshot (no slime tutorials here; the show has enough slime as it is.) Keep reading for more information, or watch the trailer below.

How to watch “The Guy Who Didn’t Like Musicals” revival

”The Guy Who Didn’t Like Musicals: Reprised!” will be available for purchase on May 11, 2026 through the Team StarKid website. You can also purchase the show’s live-recorded cast album ahead of time. Most StarKid shows are eventually available for free on YouTube; this has been true since their early days making “A Very Potter Musical.” And this show is no exception. You can already watch the original “Guy Who Didn’t Like Musicals” for free on YouTube right now, or you could wait and watch “TGWDLM: Reprised!” when it comes to YouTube on July 4, 2026.

What’s next for StarKid?

StarKid is already working on their next show—not another Hatchetfield installment, but a new story inspired by games like Dungeons & Dragons. It’s called “Tomb Quest,” and it’s crowdfunding now on Kickstarter. According to the campaign description, “Tomb Quest” is a “dungeon-delving musical comedy” about five friends reunited to finish their old high school TTRPG campaign. As of writing, “Tomb Quest” has raised about $221,000 of its $275,000 goal.

Who we are

At CableTV.com, we’re not just entertainment experts—we’re also fans just like you. So while our first priority is reporting the best and most accurate information about TV providers and streaming services, we also love talking about our favorite shows and movies. So you can count on us to share our honest opinions and unbridled enthusiasm. For more on our process, check out our Editorial Policy.
